wa_artic_rose.name: Half Moon
wa_artic_rose.note: True summit of Half Moon on Kangaroo Ridge; final moves are class 5+ rock.

wa_half_fast.name: Half Moon
wa_half_fast.note: True summit of Half Moon on Kangaroo Ridge; final moves are class 5+ rock.

wa_east_ridge_3.name: Silver Star Mountain (Okanogan) Summit
wa_east_ridge_3.note: True summit of Silver Star, reached via the East Ridge (5.9+) from the Burgundy Col/Silver Star Glacier approach near Washington Pass; trailhead pullout coordinates could not be reliably pinned down, so only the verified summit point is included.

wa_east_ridge_6.name: PCT Trailhead (Alpental Rd / Exit 52)
wa_east_ridge_6.note: Start of the Pacific Crest Trail north from Snoqualmie Pass toward Kendall Katwalk and Bumblebee Pass.

wa_east_ridge_6.name: Mount Thomson Summit
wa_east_ridge_6.note: Reached via PCT to Bumblebee Pass, then a basin south of the peak; East Ridge finishes with a short Class 4 step below the top.

wa_east_ridge_direct.name: Boston Basin Trailhead (Cascade River Road)
wa_east_ridge_direct.note: Small pullout on Cascade River Road opposite Johannesburg Mountain, just past mile 22.

wa_east_ridge_direct.name: Boston Basin High Camp
wa_east_ridge_direct.note: High camp used for Forbidden Peak and Torment climbs; snow can linger here into July.

wa_east_ridge_direct.name: Forbidden Peak Summit
wa_east_ridge_direct.note: East Ridge Direct trends east off the West Ridge approach onto a broad shoulder before finishing up the ridge, Grade III 5.7-5.8.

wa_east_wilmans_spire.name: Barlow Pass Trailhead
wa_east_wilmans_spire.note: Mountain Loop Highway trailhead; hike/bike the closed road toward Monte Cristo.

wa_east_wilmans_spire.name: Monte Cristo Townsite
wa_east_wilmans_spire.note: Old mining townsite; pick up the Glacier Basin Trail here toward the Wilmans Spires.

wa_east_wilmans_spire.name: East Wilmans Spire
wa_east_wilmans_spire.note: Most-climbed of the three Wilmans Spires above Glacier Basin; best climbed early season while the approach gully still holds snow.

wa_elephant_butte_standard_route.name: Sourdough Mountain Trailhead (Diablo)
wa_elephant_butte_standard_route.note: Trail starts behind the Diablo swimming pool off SR-20 milepost 126.

wa_elephant_butte_standard_route.name: Elephant Butte Summit
wa_elephant_butte_standard_route.note: Long multi-day ridge traverse via Sourdough/Stetattle Ridge; a significant drop and reclimb separates the ridge from the Elephant Butte summit.

wa_ez_way.name: Elk Pass / Boundary Trailhead
wa_ez_way.note: Boundary Trail #1 access off FR-25, driving distance further via FR-2551 spur toward Shark Rock.

wa_ez_way.name: Shark Rock (EZ Way topout)
wa_ez_way.note: Dark Divide formation midway between Mt St Helens and Mt Adams; EZ Way is rated 5.6.

wa_frying_pan_whitman_glaciers.name: Fryingpan Creek Trailhead
wa_frying_pan_whitman_glaciers.note: Wonderland Trail junction near the White River entrance, Mount Rainier NP.

wa_frying_pan_whitman_glaciers.name: Little Tahoma Summit
wa_frying_pan_whitman_glaciers.note: Via Fryingpan Glacier, Whitman Notch (~9100 ft), and the Whitman Glacier to the East Ridge; glacier permit required.

wa_gato_negro.name: Blue Lake Trailhead (Washington Pass)
wa_gato_negro.note: Standard access point for the Wine Spires cirque off Highway 20.

wa_gato_negro.name: Whine Spire (Gato Negro topout)
wa_gato_negro.note: 12-pitch alpine trad route (5.10a/b) on the sub-peak east of Silver Star Mountain; topout elevation is approximate.

wa_honeymoon_route.name: Royal Lake
wa_honeymoon_route.note: Via Upper Dungeness Trail from Royal Basin trailhead; exact trailhead coordinates could not be confidently verified so only Royal Lake and the summit are included.

wa_honeymoon_route.name: Mount Deception Summit
wa_honeymoon_route.note: Highest peak of the eastern Olympics; rock is notoriously loose, early-season (pre-July) climbing strongly preferred.

wa_hourglass_gully_winter.name: Lake Serene Trailhead
wa_hourglass_gully_winter.note: US-2 trailhead near Index used to approach Mount Index's Main Peak.

wa_hourglass_gully_winter.name: Mount Index Main Peak Summit
wa_hourglass_gully_winter.note: Winter/spring line up the Hourglass Gully; stay left of the island in the gully to avoid the sun-exposed, rockfall-prone right side.

wa_j_tnar.name: Lake Serene Trailhead
wa_j_tnar.note: Same US-2 trailhead used for Mount Index; from Lake Serene traverse talus to the Middle Peak cirque.

wa_j_tnar.name: Jötunheim Wall Topout (Middle Peak)
wa_j_tnar.note: 16-pitch, Grade VI big wall (5.9 A3+) with bolted anchors and portaledge bivies; best climbed late July-early Sept before snowmelt turns pitches into waterfalls.

wa_junior_s_farm.name: Tyler Peak Trailhead
wa_junior_s_farm.note: Large parking area just past the Dungeness River bridge on FR-2870.

wa_junior_s_farm.name: The Full Montey (Junior's Farm topout)
wa_junior_s_farm.note: 8-pitch sport/alpine route (5.9) at Tyler Peak Crags in Olympic National Forest; topout elevation is approximate.

wa_lewis_creek_route.name: Barclay Lake Trailhead
wa_lewis_creek_route.note: US-2 trailhead near Baring, off FR-6024.

wa_lewis_creek_route.name: Gunn Peak Summit
wa_lewis_creek_route.note: Class 3-4 scramble with a notoriously hard-to-follow approach and a narrow, exposed summit ledge on the north side.

wa_mount_maude_r1.name: Cottonwood Campground (Entiat River Road)
wa_mount_maude_r1.note: End of pavement on Entiat River Road; Entiat River Trail continues from here.

wa_mount_maude_r1.name: Mount Maude Summit
wa_mount_maude_r1.note: Entiat River Trail to Entiat Meadows, then a descending glacier traverse to the base of the North Face; long, cornice-threatened snow/ice face best climbed before mid-July.

wa_mount_st_helens_worm_flows.name: Marble Mountain Sno-Park
wa_mount_st_helens_worm_flows.note: Standard winter/spring trailhead; climbing permit required above 4,800 ft year-round.

wa_mount_st_helens_worm_flows.name: Mount St. Helens Crater Rim
wa_mount_st_helens_worm_flows.note: Breaks treeline near 4,600 ft, joins the Monitor Ridge route near 7,000 ft, then follows the crater rim west to the true high point.

wa_north_face_left_buttress.name: Castle Peak Summit
wa_north_face_left_buttress.note: Remote Pasayten peak on the US/Canada border; multi-day approach from either Cathedral Provincial Park (BC) or Robinson Creek could not be pinned to a reliable single trailhead coordinate, so only the summit is included.

wa_north_face_of_the_mole.name: Alphabet Rock (Icicle Creek Road)
wa_north_face_of_the_mole.note: Roadside parking used to access the Hook Creek Drainage spires above Icicle Creek.

wa_north_face_of_the_mole.name: The Mole (Edward Peak) North Face topout
wa_north_face_of_the_mole.note: USGS name for this formation is Edward Peak; North Face follows an obvious crack system, 5.7, with a 3-rappel descent to the east.

wa_north_ridge_west_side.name: Rainy Pass PCT Trailhead
wa_north_ridge_west_side.note: PCT north trailhead off Highway 20 at Rainy Pass.

wa_north_ridge_west_side.name: Molar Tooth
wa_north_ridge_west_side.note: Follow the PCT ~1.5 mi to near Porcupine Creek, then ascend the ridge NE to the peak between Cutthroat Pass and Cutthroat Peak.

wa_northeast_buttress_3.name: Alpental / Snow Lake Trailhead
wa_northeast_buttress_3.note: Upper Alpental ski area parking lot, off I-90 exit 52.

wa_northeast_buttress_3.name: Chair Peak NE Buttress Base
wa_northeast_buttress_3.note: Basin below Chair Peak's east face; climb a short snow gully to gain the ridge that forms the Northeast Buttress (classic winter ice/mixed line).

wa_northeast_face_direct.name: Cascade Pass Trailhead
wa_northeast_face_direct.note: End of Cascade River Road, 23 miles from Marblemount.

wa_northeast_face_direct.name: Mount Formidable Summit
wa_northeast_face_direct.note: Via Cascade Pass, the Ptarmigan Traverse, and the Middle/Formidable Glaciers; moderate rock to 5.6 with an AI2+ pitch in a hidden couloir.

wa_northeast_ridge_1963_route.name: Cascade Pass Trailhead
wa_northeast_ridge_1963_route.note: End of Cascade River Road; Johannesburg's NE face towers directly overhead.

wa_northeast_ridge_1963_route.name: Cascade Pass
wa_northeast_ridge_1963_route.note: Maintained trail tops out here; leave it for the climbers' path toward Johannesburg.

wa_northeast_ridge_1963_route.name: Cascade River Crossing / Terminal Moraine
wa_northeast_ridge_1963_route.note: Off-trail descent and ford of the infant Cascade River below the terminal moraine.

wa_northeast_ridge_1963_route.name: Base of C-J Couloir
wa_northeast_ridge_1963_route.note: Gain rock just before the Cascade-Johannesburg couloir narrows; the NE Ridge (1963) climbs the rib right of the couloir.

wa_northeast_ridge_1963_route.name: Hanging Glacier Traverse
wa_northeast_ridge_1963_route.note: Exposed rising traverse beneath Johannesburg's hanging glaciers; rockfall and icefall hazard.

wa_northeast_ridge_1963_route.name: Johannesburg Mountain
wa_northeast_ridge_1963_route.note: Summit via the Northeast Ridge (1963 Route).

wa_nw_ridge.name: Thirtymile Trailhead
wa_nw_ridge.note: End of FR-51 on the west side of the Chewuch River; standard start for the Remmel approach.

wa_nw_ridge.name: Four Point Lake
wa_nw_ridge.note: Multiple good campsites with views of Remmel; standard bivy.

wa_nw_ridge.name: NW Ridge Camp
wa_nw_ridge.note: Bivy on Remmel's NW Ridge itself, reached via ridge travel from the Four Point Lake basin.

wa_nw_ridge.name: Mt Remmel
wa_nw_ridge.note: Summit via the NW Ridge; straightforward class 2-3 on good-to-very-good rock.

wa_nw_ridge_2.name: Stuart/Colchuck Trail Split
wa_nw_ridge_2.note: Colchuck Lake Trail breaks left from the Stuart Lake Trail.

wa_nw_ridge_2.name: Colchuck Lake
wa_nw_ridge_2.note: Popular camp/bivy at the lake outlet, below Dragontail and Colchuck Peak.

wa_nw_ridge_2.name: Aasgard Pass Gully Break
wa_nw_ridge_2.note: Leave the Aasgard Pass boot path ~400 ft above the lake, traverse brush to the gully leading to Balanced Rock.

wa_poltergeist_pinnacle.name: Hannegan Trailhead
wa_poltergeist_pinnacle.note: End of Ruth Creek Road (FR 32) at Hannegan Campground.

wa_poltergeist_pinnacle.name: Hannegan Pass
wa_poltergeist_pinnacle.note: Trail crests here; continue cross-country toward the Chilliwack drainage and Whatcom Pass.

wa_poltergeist_pinnacle.name: Boundary Camp
wa_poltergeist_pinnacle.note: Camp in the Chilliwack River valley below the climb to Whatcom Pass.

wa_poltergeist_pinnacle.name: Whatcom Pass
wa_poltergeist_pinnacle.note: Gap between Whatcom Peak and the Challenger massif; gateway to Challenger Glacier.

wa_poltergeist_pinnacle.name: Challenger Glacier Crossing
wa_poltergeist_pinnacle.note: Roped glacier travel across Challenger Glacier; crevasse hazard.

wa_poltergeist_pinnacle.name: Poltergeist Pinnacle
wa_poltergeist_pinnacle.note: Subsidiary spire just south of Mount Challenger's main summit, between Challenger and Crooked Thumb.

wa_ptarmigan_traverse.name: Cascade Pass Trailhead
wa_ptarmigan_traverse.note: North end of the traverse; end of Cascade River Road.

wa_ptarmigan_traverse.name: Cascade Pass
wa_ptarmigan_traverse.note: Maintained trail ends here; traverse continues cross-country.

wa_ptarmigan_traverse.name: Cache Col
wa_ptarmigan_traverse.note: Glacier-travel notch south of Cascade Pass; gateway to the traverse proper.

wa_ptarmigan_traverse.name: Kool-Aid Lake
wa_ptarmigan_traverse.note: First bivy; the exposed 'Red Ledges' traverse below Art's Knoll follows.

wa_ptarmigan_traverse.name: Red Ledges (Art's Knoll)
wa_ptarmigan_traverse.note: Narrow, exposed ledge crossing; a fall here would be serious.

wa_ptarmigan_traverse.name: Yang-Yang Lakes
wa_ptarmigan_traverse.note: Second common bivy, below Spider-Formidable Col.

wa_ptarmigan_traverse.name: Spider-Formidable Col
wa_ptarmigan_traverse.note: High col crossing toward the LeConte Glacier and Sentinel/Old Guard Peaks.

wa_ptarmigan_traverse.name: White Rock Lakes / South Cascade Glacier Col
wa_ptarmigan_traverse.note: Third bivy; crosses the flat South Cascade Glacier en route to Spire Point.

wa_ptarmigan_traverse.name: Spire Point
wa_ptarmigan_traverse.note: Prominent landmark near the Dome-Dana col.

wa_ptarmigan_traverse.name: Cub Lake / Itswoot Ridge
wa_ptarmigan_traverse.note: Fourth bivy on Itswoot Ridge above Cub and Itswoot Lakes.

wa_ptarmigan_traverse.name: Downey Creek Trailhead
wa_ptarmigan_traverse.note: Southern terminus on Suiattle River Road; well-maintained trail out.

wa_ridge_traverse_from_east_fury.name: Big Beaver Trailhead (Ross Lake)
wa_ridge_traverse_from_east_fury.note: Reached by trail or water-taxi along Ross Lake.

wa_ridge_traverse_from_east_fury.name: Access Creek Junction
wa_ridge_traverse_from_east_fury.note: Leave Big Beaver Trail to ascend Access Creek toward the Southern Pickets.

wa_ridge_traverse_from_east_fury.name: Access Col
wa_ridge_traverse_from_east_fury.note: Gains the crest between Access Creek and Luna Cirque.

wa_ridge_traverse_from_east_fury.name: Luna Col
wa_ridge_traverse_from_east_fury.note: High col below Luna Peak; standard approach to the Fury massif.

wa_ridge_traverse_from_east_fury.name: East Fury
wa_ridge_traverse_from_east_fury.note: Start of the ridge traverse to West Fury.

wa_ridge_traverse_from_east_fury.name: Loose Tower Traverse
wa_ridge_traverse_from_east_fury.note: Descend to a low saddle then climb over three loose towers, low 5th class; rope recommended for rappels.

wa_ridge_traverse_from_east_fury.name: West Fury (Mount Fury)
wa_ridge_traverse_from_east_fury.note: Higher, officially-named summit of Mount Fury.

wa_ruth_icy_traverse.name: Hannegan Trailhead
wa_ruth_icy_traverse.note: End of Ruth Creek Road (FR 32) at Hannegan Campground.

wa_ruth_icy_traverse.name: Hannegan Pass
wa_ruth_icy_traverse.note: Leave the maintained trail; climbers path heads east/south around a shoulder toward Ruth Glacier.

wa_ruth_icy_traverse.name: Ruth Glacier Camp
wa_ruth_icy_traverse.note: Common high camp on Ruth Mountain's north slope.

wa_ruth_icy_traverse.name: Ruth-Icy Saddle
wa_ruth_icy_traverse.note: Descend a west-facing snow slope from Ruth to this connecting saddle.

wa_ruth_icy_traverse.name: Icy Peak
wa_ruth_icy_traverse.note: Glacier climb up the NW slope, then a talus/snow gully to the summit horn; rap sling on a horn near the top.

wa_scramble_route.name: Eagle Peak Trailhead
wa_scramble_route.note: Near Longmire; cross the suspension bridge and walk back to the trail start.

wa_scramble_route.name: Eagle Peak Saddle
wa_scramble_route.note: High point of the maintained trail; sweeping views of Mount Rainier, Adams, and St. Helens.

wa_scramble_route.name: Eagle Peak
wa_scramble_route.note: Class III-IV scramble beyond the saddle; loose rock and exposure.

wa_slippery_slab_tower_ne_face.name: Surprise Creek Trailhead
wa_slippery_slab_tower_ne_face.note: 0.3 mi off US-2, ~10 mi east of Skykomish.

wa_slippery_slab_tower_ne_face.name: Surprise Lake
wa_slippery_slab_tower_ne_face.note: Trail switchbacks above the lake to join the PCT.

wa_slippery_slab_tower_ne_face.name: Trap Pass
wa_slippery_slab_tower_ne_face.note: PCT junction; the climbers path to Thunder Mountain and Slippery Slab Tower leaves directly from the top of the pass.

wa_slippery_slab_tower_ne_face.name: Base of Slippery Slab Tower
wa_slippery_slab_tower_ne_face.note: Belay tree at the start of Pitch 1 of the NE Face.

wa_slippery_slab_tower_ne_face.name: Slippery Slab Tower NE Face Topout
wa_slippery_slab_tower_ne_face.note: Two-pitch 5.4; Pitch 2 diagonals left into a gully to a large boulder near the top.

wa_the_devils_club.name: Depot Creek Trailhead
wa_the_devils_club.note: Off Chilliwack Lake Road/Depot Creek Road in Canada; crosses the border approach to Southeast Mox.

wa_the_devils_club.name: Depot Creek Falls
wa_the_devils_club.note: Steep, ice-cold ford-and-bushwhack section past the falls.

wa_the_devils_club.name: Ouzel Lake
wa_the_devils_club.note: Common camp before the Redoubt Glacier.

wa_the_devils_club.name: Redoubt Glacier Camp
wa_the_devils_club.note: Flat bivy on the Redoubt Glacier used to access the Mox massif.

wa_the_devils_club.name: Base of East Face Headwall
wa_the_devils_club.note: Bivy ledge below the 1,500-ft near-vertical headwall of the Devil's Club route.

wa_the_devils_club.name: Southeast Mox Peak
wa_the_devils_club.note: Top of the 2,500-ft east face, one of the last great problems of the Pickets.

wa_west_ridge_4.name: Middle Fork Nooksack Trailhead
wa_west_ridge_4.note: Gated bridge across the Middle Fork Nooksack River off Porter Creek Road (FR 38).

wa_west_ridge_4.name: South Twin Approach Basin
wa_west_ridge_4.note: Talus and heather basin below the West Ridge.

wa_west_ridge_4.name: South Twin
wa_west_ridge_4.note: Highest of the Twin Sisters; West Ridge is the standard scramble route on solid olivine rock.

wa_west_ridge_5.name: Middle Fork Nooksack Trailhead
wa_west_ridge_5.note: Gated bridge across the Middle Fork Nooksack River off Porter Creek Road (FR 38).

wa_west_ridge_5.name: North Twin Approach Basin
wa_west_ridge_5.note: Basin below North Twin's craggy West Ridge.

wa_west_ridge_5.name: North Twin
wa_west_ridge_5.note: West Ridge is a narrow, popular scramble on solid olivine rock.

wa_south_face.name: Early Winters Creek Trailhead
wa_south_face.note: Wide pullout on Hwy 20, ~4 mi east of Washington Pass; faint trail descends to Early Winters Creek.

wa_south_face.name: The Ledge Bivy
wa_south_face.note: Common bivy area partway up to Burgundy Col.

wa_south_face.name: Burgundy Col
wa_south_face.note: Separates the Wine Spires from Vasiliki Ridge; Vasiliki Tower stands on the left entering the col.

wa_south_face.name: Vasiliki Tower
wa_south_face.note: South Face route, just north of Burgundy Col.

wa_vanishing_point.name: Barclay Lake Trailhead
wa_vanishing_point.note: Shared trailhead for Barclay Lake and Baring Mountain approaches.

wa_vanishing_point.name: Barclay Lake
wa_vanishing_point.note: Trail continues past the lake toward Baring's north side.

wa_vanishing_point.name: Dolomite Tower Base
wa_vanishing_point.note: Climbers trail circles around most of Dolomite Tower before reaching the gully separating it from Baring Mountain.

wa_vanishing_point.name: Vanishing Point Topout
wa_vanishing_point.note: Grade VI 5.12b established over many bolted pitches in the late 1990s; starts left of the prominent arete.

wa_southwest_rib_2.name: Crystal Creek Junction
wa_southwest_rib_2.note: Leave Ingalls Creek Trail ~100 ft before it crosses Crystal Creek.

wa_southwest_rib_2.name: Crystal Lake
wa_southwest_rib_2.note: Basin camp below the SW Rib; also reachable descending from the Enchantments plateau.

wa_southwest_rib_2.name: Crystal Lake Tower
wa_southwest_rib_2.note: Westernmost point on McClellan Peak's summit ridge; SW Rib forms a continuous ~1,400-ft line down into Crystal Creek Basin.

wa_smears_jugs_and_rock_roll.name: Snow Lakes Trailhead
wa_smears_jugs_and_rock_roll.note: Standard entry to the Enchantments core zone via Snow Lakes Trail #1553.

wa_smears_jugs_and_rock_roll.name: Snow Lakes
wa_smears_jugs_and_rock_roll.note: Trail continues up past the lakes toward Enchantment Basin.

wa_smears_jugs_and_rock_roll.name: Viviane Campsite (Lake Viviane)
wa_smears_jugs_and_rock_roll.note: Reached after climbing ~6,400 vertical ft; limited campsites on either side of the lake outlet, adjacent to the crag.

wa_west_face_3.name: Joe Lake
wa_west_face_3.note: PCT rounds Alaska Mountain's east ridge and drops to the west end of Joe Lake.

wa_west_face_3.name: West Face Talus Basin
wa_west_face_3.note: Flowery meadow northeast of Joe Lake; head up talus into the basin below the west face.

wa_west_face_3.name: Huckleberry Mountain
wa_west_face_3.note: West Face route, first climbed September 2001; stiff class 3-4 summit block.

wa_gorillas_direct.name: Esmeralda Basin Trailhead (North Fork Teanaway Rd)
wa_gorillas_direct.note: Ingalls Way trail via Esmeralda Basin; shared approach for the Gorillas routes on Stuart's south/west side.

wa_gorillas_direct.name: Ingalls Pass
wa_gorillas_direct.note: Trail crest crossing en route toward Stuart Pass/Goat Pass and the West Face Wall between them.

wa_gorillas_direct.name: Mount Stuart summit area
wa_gorillas_direct.note: Route climbs the ~1000' West Face Wall between Stuart Pass and Goat Pass, topping out on the upper West Ridge near the true summit.

wa_gorillas_in_the_mist.name: Esmeralda Basin Trailhead (North Fork Teanaway Rd)
wa_gorillas_in_the_mist.note: Ingalls Way trail via Esmeralda Basin; shared approach for the Gorillas routes.

wa_gorillas_in_the_mist.name: Ingalls Pass
wa_gorillas_in_the_mist.note: En route toward Stuart Pass/Goat Pass.

wa_gorillas_in_the_mist.name: Mount Stuart summit area
wa_gorillas_in_the_mist.note: IV+ 5.11 line on the 1000' buttress midway between Stuart Pass and Goat Pass; tops out on the upper West Ridge near the true summit.

wa_king_kong_gorillas_direct_direct.name: Esmeralda Basin Trailhead (North Fork Teanaway Rd)
wa_king_kong_gorillas_direct_direct.note: Same approach as other Gorillas-wall routes.

wa_king_kong_gorillas_direct_direct.name: Mount Stuart summit area
wa_king_kong_gorillas_direct_direct.note: Direct-start/direct-finish variation of Gorillas Direct on the same West Face Wall; tops out near the true summit.

wa_sherpa_glacier.name: Stuart Lake Trailhead (Eightmile Creek Rd #7601)
wa_sherpa_glacier.note: Trail #1599 up Icicle/Eightmile Creek; leave the trail near the Mountaineer Creek basin to reach the glacier.

wa_sherpa_glacier.name: Sherpa Glacier toe/icefall
wa_sherpa_glacier.note: Glacier terminates as an icefall here; crevasses present, cross high where the glacier is flatter.

wa_sherpa_glacier.name: Top of Sherpa Glacier
wa_sherpa_glacier.note: Tops out at the notch gaining Mount Stuart's upper North Ridge.

wa_south_headwall.name: Esmeralda Basin Trailhead (North Fork Teanaway Rd)
wa_south_headwall.note: Ingalls Way trail approach to the south side of the mountain.

wa_south_headwall.name: Mount Stuart summit area
wa_south_headwall.note: 4-5 pitches of low 5th/4th class up the steep headwall below the summit; tops out very near and just SW of the true summit.

wa_the_direct_north_ridge_w_gendarme.name: Stuart Lake Trailhead (Eightmile Creek Rd #7601)
wa_the_direct_north_ridge_w_gendarme.note: Trail #1599 up Icicle/Eightmile Creek, then cross-country up Mountaineer Creek to the base of the ridge.

wa_the_direct_north_ridge_w_gendarme.name: Base of North Ridge (glacier moat)
wa_the_direct_north_ridge_w_gendarme.note: Gain the ridge crest via the moat beside the Sherpa/Ice Cliff Glaciers; complete ridge route starts here.

wa_the_direct_north_ridge_w_gendarme.name: Mount Stuart summit
wa_the_direct_north_ridge_w_gendarme.note: Crux is the Great Gendarme (5.9 offwidth) about 500 ft below the summit; a short rappel bypasses it around the base.

wa_upper_north_ridge_w_great_gendarme.name: Stuart Lake Trailhead (Eightmile Creek Rd #7601)
wa_upper_north_ridge_w_great_gendarme.note: Trail #1599 up Icicle/Eightmile Creek to the Mountaineer Creek basin.

wa_upper_north_ridge_w_great_gendarme.name: Top of Sherpa Glacier notch
wa_upper_north_ridge_w_great_gendarme.note: Ascend Sherpa Glacier to this notch to gain the upper North Ridge, bypassing the lower ridge's early pitches.

wa_upper_north_ridge_w_great_gendarme.name: Mount Stuart summit
wa_upper_north_ridge_w_great_gendarme.note: Crux is the Great Gendarme (5.9 offwidth) about 500 ft below the summit.

wa_ne_ridge.name: Andrews Creek Trailhead
wa_ne_ridge.note: Long approach (~19-20 mi) via Andrews Creek Trail, Andrews Pass, and the Boundary Trail to the Upper Cathedral Lake basin below Cathedral Peak.

wa_ne_ridge.name: Cathedral Peak summit
wa_ne_ridge.note: NE Ridge climbs directly to the true summit.

wa_south_face_10.name: Andrews Creek Trailhead
wa_south_face_10.note: Same long Pasayten Wilderness approach as other Cathedral Peak routes.

wa_south_face_10.name: Cathedral Peak summit
wa_south_face_10.note: Route climbs the ~1000' South Face wall, topping out at/near the true summit.

wa_the_monk_le_gibet.name: Andrews Creek Trailhead
wa_the_monk_le_gibet.note: Long approach via Andrews Creek/Andrews Pass/Boundary Trail to Upper Cathedral Lake, then ~40 min to the base of The Monk.

wa_the_monk_le_gibet.name: The Monk (tower top)
wa_the_monk_le_gibet.note: The Monk is a semi-detached ~1000' tower leaning against Cathedral Peak's flank, in the gully between the two formations.

wa_the_monk_odine.name: Andrews Creek Trailhead
wa_the_monk_odine.note: Same approach as other Monk routes.

wa_the_monk_scabo.name: Andrews Creek Trailhead
wa_the_monk_scabo.note: Same approach as other Monk routes.

wa_the_monk_west_cracks_left_crack.name: Andrews Creek Trailhead
wa_the_monk_west_cracks_left_crack.note: Same approach as other Monk routes.

wa_the_monk_west_cracks_left_crack.name: The Monk (tower top)
wa_the_monk_west_cracks_left_crack.note: Left of the two West Cracks lines on The Monk's west side.

wa_the_monk_west_cracks_right_crack.name: Andrews Creek Trailhead
wa_the_monk_west_cracks_right_crack.note: Same approach as other Monk routes.

wa_the_monk_west_cracks_right_crack.name: The Monk (tower top)
wa_the_monk_west_cracks_right_crack.note: Right of the two West Cracks lines on The Monk's west side.

wa_alice_in_wonderland.name: Highway 20 pullout near Mazama (below Spires Gully)
wa_alice_in_wonderland.note: Roadside pullout on the North Cascades Highway; descend, cross Early Winters Creek, and contour ~20-25 min to the crag.

wa_alice_in_wonderland.name: Summertime Crag
wa_alice_in_wonderland.note: South-facing ~80-degree bolted face; shaded until mid-afternoon in summer.

wa_caravan.name: Highway 20 pullout near Mazama (below Spires Gully)
wa_caravan.note: Roadside pullout; ~20-25 min approach crossing Early Winters Creek.

wa_chitlins_con_carne.name: Highway 20 pullout near Mazama (below Spires Gully)
wa_chitlins_con_carne.note: Roadside pullout; ~20-25 min approach crossing Early Winters Creek.

wa_django.name: Highway 20 pullout near Mazama (below Spires Gully)
wa_django.note: Roadside pullout; ~20-25 min approach crossing Early Winters Creek.

wa_hottentot.name: Highway 20 pullout near Mazama (below Spires Gully)
wa_hottentot.note: Roadside pullout; ~20-25 min approach crossing Early Winters Creek.

wa_summertime.name: Highway 20 pullout near Mazama (below Spires Gully)
wa_summertime.note: Roadside pullout; ~20-25 min approach crossing Early Winters Creek.

wa_bowling_alley_aka_regular_route.name: Pinto Rock base (end of NF-77)
wa_bowling_alley_aka_regular_route.note: Approach via NF-25 to Mosquito Meadows, then NF-28/NF-77 (rough road) to the base of the ~400' cliff, Gifford Pinchot National Forest.

wa_bowling_alley_aka_regular_route.name: Pinto Rock summit
wa_bowling_alley_aka_regular_route.note: 5-pitch regular route through a basalt band and chimney to the summit; descend via 2 raps (60m) or 1 double-rope rap off the north end of the east face.

wa_cobbles_101.name: Pinto Rock base (end of NF-77)
wa_cobbles_101.note: Same forest-road approach as Bowling Alley.

wa_cobbles_101.name: Pinto Rock summit
wa_cobbles_101.note: Starts on the prow left of the Bowling Alley gully; 5.7, shares the same summit/rappel descent.

wa_austera_peak.name: Eldorado Creek / Cascade River Road TH
wa_austera_peak.note: Large pullout at mile 20 of Cascade River Road; cross the Cascade River on a log/flagged crossing to the climbers' trail.

wa_austera_peak.name: Eldorado (East Ridge) camp
wa_austera_peak.note: High camp on the Inspiration Glacier flat used as base for Eldorado's satellite peaks.

wa_austera_peak.name: Klawatti col
wa_austera_peak.note: Break in the ridge above the McAllister Glacier; rappel ~75 ft to the Klawatti Glacier.

wa_austera_peak.name: Klawatti Glacier crevasses
wa_austera_peak.note: Traverse high and left across the crevassed Klawatti Glacier toward Austera.

wa_austera_peak.name: Austera Peak
wa_austera_peak.note: Final 30 ft of low 5th-class climbing on the northeast face.

wa_klawatti_peak_sw_buttress.name: Eldorado Creek / Cascade River Road TH
Klawatti camp / col
wa_klawatti_peak_sw_buttress.note: Camp south of Klawatti near the col dividing the Klawatti and McAllister Glaciers; SW Buttress start is minutes away.

wa_klawatti_peak_sw_buttress.name: Klawatti Peak
wa_klawatti_peak_sw_buttress.note: SW Buttress: 2-3 short low-5th-class pitches above the moat to the summit.

wa_tepeh_towers.name: Eldorado Creek / Cascade River Road TH
Base of Tepeh Towers
wa_tepeh_towers.note: ~45 min glacier walk N/NW from camp to where the towers rise off the Eldorado ice cap.

wa_tepeh_towers.name: Tepeh Towers
wa_tepeh_towers.note: One short low-5th-class pitch above the moat, then a scramble to the top.

wa_north_ridge_5.name: Eldorado Creek / Cascade River Road TH
Base of North Ridge
wa_north_ridge_5.note: Traverse the upper Eldorado/Inspiration Glacier to the foot of the ridge.

wa_north_ridge_5.name: Eldorado Peak
wa_north_ridge_5.note: Airy, exposed 5.6 ridge crest of solid granite to the summit.

wa_south_ridge_4.name: Eldorado Creek / Cascade River Road TH
Roush Creek Basin / Eldorado Glacier toe
wa_south_ridge_4.note: Snowfields lead onto the Eldorado Glacier, staying left (west) of the rocky ridge.

wa_south_ridge_4.name: Eldorado camp (Inspiration Glacier flat)
wa_south_ridge_4.note: Good camps on rocks near the broad flat of the Inspiration Glacier.

wa_south_ridge_4.name: Eldorado Peak
wa_south_ridge_4.note: Broad, snow-covered ridge to the spectacular knife-edge summit; the standard/easiest route.

wa_west_ridge_7.name: Eldorado Creek / Cascade River Road TH
Glacier camp below the West Arete
wa_west_ridge_7.note: Camp on the Eldorado Glacier at the base of the arete.

wa_west_ridge_7.name: Eldorado Peak
wa_west_ridge_7.note: Nearly 3,000 ft of continuous 5.9 rock from glacier to summit — among the longest rock climbs in the North Cascades.

wa_beckey_schmidtke.name: White Salmon Road (hairpin) TH
wa_beckey_schmidtke.note: Small pullout on Mt Baker Highway (SR 542) just below the White Salmon Day Lodge.

wa_beckey_schmidtke.name: Price Glacier icefall
wa_beckey_schmidtke.note: Steepest, most heavily crevassed glacier on Shuksan; gains the base of Nooksack Tower.

wa_beckey_schmidtke.name: Nooksack Tower
wa_beckey_schmidtke.note: Beckey-Schmidtke (1946 FA) climbs the tower's buttress in ~10 pitches to the summit of Nooksack Tower, a subsidiary summit of the Shuksan massif.

wa_mount_shuksan_north_face.name: Base of North Face
wa_mount_shuksan_north_face.note: Snow/glacier shoulder dividing the White Salmon cirque from the Price Glacier cirque.

wa_mount_shuksan_north_face.name: North Shoulder
wa_mount_shuksan_north_face.note: Tops out on the shoulder NE of the summit pyramid near the Crystal Glacier.

wa_mount_shuksan_white_salmon_glacier.name: White Salmon Glacier toe
wa_mount_shuksan_white_salmon_glacier.note: Glacier travel begins where the road/approach ends.

wa_mount_shuksan_white_salmon_glacier.name: Winnie's Slide
wa_mount_shuksan_white_salmon_glacier.note: 150-200 ft step of steep 40-45° snow/ice onto the Upper Curtis Glacier; schrund can be tricky late season.

wa_mount_shuksan_white_salmon_glacier.name: Hell's Highway
wa_mount_shuksan_white_salmon_glacier.note: Snow chute connecting to the west edge of the Sulphide Glacier.

wa_southeast_ridge_se_corner.name: Shannon Ridge TH
wa_southeast_ridge_se_corner.note: End of FR 1152-014 spur off Baker Lake Road.

wa_southeast_ridge_se_corner.name: Sulphide Glacier camp
wa_southeast_ridge_se_corner.note: Camp at the pass where Shannon Ridge's flat crest meets the glacier.

wa_southeast_ridge_se_corner.name: Notch below SE Ridge
wa_southeast_ridge_se_corner.note: Trend right off the Sulphide Glacier onto the ridge; ~600 vertical ft of 5.3-5.7 to the summit.

wa_sw_couloir_and_face.name: Lake Ann TH
wa_sw_couloir_and_face.note: Paved lot on SR 542 just below Artist Point/Austin Pass.

wa_sw_couloir_and_face.name: Lower Curtis Glacier icefall base
wa_sw_couloir_and_face.note: From below Fisher Chimneys, traverse right (east) across the Lower Curtis Glacier to the icefall.

wa_sw_couloir_and_face.name: Ice chimney / headwall
wa_sw_couloir_and_face.note: Vertical ice chimney in the lower icefall, the technical crux (WI2-3).

wa_chockstone_route.name: Blue Lake TH
wa_chockstone_route.note: SR 20 pullout 1.5 mi west of Washington Pass.

wa_chockstone_route.name: Spire col (gully base)
wa_chockstone_route.note: Base of the gully between North and South Early Winters Spires, below the giant chockstones.

wa_chockstone_route.name: Chockstone crux
wa_chockstone_route.note: 5.7 pitch bypassing the first giant chockstone on the left.

wa_early_winter_couloir.name: Spire Gully entrance
wa_early_winter_couloir.note: Ascend Spire Gully then traverse north to the base of the couloir's snow ramp.

wa_early_winter_couloir.name: Summit cornice
wa_early_winter_couloir.note: Large cornice guards the top of the couloir; often requires aid/mixed moves and has no fixed rap anchors.

wa_flycatcher_buttress.name: Base of Flycatcher Buttress
wa_flycatcher_buttress.note: Stay right of the gully dividing the two spires, scrambling to a flat ledge at the start.

wa_labor_pains.name: Chockstone Route start (shared)
wa_labor_pains.note: Shares its start with the Chockstone Route before breaking left onto steeper 5.11a terrain.

wa_the_west_face.name: Base of West Face
wa_the_west_face.note: Low-angle cracks lead to a tree-topped chimney on the left side of the west face; shares the start with the NW Corner.

wa_finger_of_fatwa.name: Andrews Creek Trailhead
wa_finger_of_fatwa.note: Standard approach to Cathedral Peak/Amphitheater Mountain via Andrews Creek Trail #504.

wa_finger_of_fatwa.name: Amphitheater Mountain
wa_finger_of_fatwa.note: North Face routes are approached from Upper Cathedral Lake/Cathedral Pass basin below this summit.

wa_middle_finger_buttress_left_side.name: Andrews Creek Trailhead
wa_middle_finger_buttress_left_side.note: Standard approach to Cathedral Peak/Amphitheater Mountain via Andrews Creek Trail #504.

wa_middle_finger_buttress_left_side.name: Amphitheater Mountain
wa_middle_finger_buttress_left_side.note: Middle Finger Buttress is on the North Face, approached from the Upper Cathedral Lake basin.

wa_middle_finger_buttress_right_side.name: Andrews Creek Trailhead
wa_middle_finger_buttress_right_side.note: Standard approach to Cathedral Peak/Amphitheater Mountain via Andrews Creek Trail #504.

wa_middle_finger_buttress_right_side.name: Amphitheater Mountain
wa_middle_finger_buttress_right_side.note: Middle Finger Buttress is on the North Face, approached from the Upper Cathedral Lake basin.

wa_north_ridge_8.name: Andrews Creek Trailhead
wa_north_ridge_8.note: Standard approach to Cathedral Peak/Amphitheater Mountain via Andrews Creek Trail #504.

wa_pilgrimage_to_mecca.name: Andrews Creek Trailhead
wa_pilgrimage_to_mecca.note: Standard approach to Cathedral Peak/Amphitheater Mountain via Andrews Creek Trail #504.

wa_pilgrimage_to_mecca.name: Amphitheater Mountain
wa_pilgrimage_to_mecca.note: 4-pitch granite route near Upper Cathedral Lake.

wa_accidental_discharge_east_face.name: Andrews Creek Trailhead
wa_accidental_discharge_east_face.note: Shared Pasayten approach with Amphitheater Mountain massif.

wa_accidental_discharge_east_face.name: South Peak (Amphitheater Mountain)
wa_accidental_discharge_east_face.note: South Peak is the highest sub-summit of the T-shaped Amphitheater Mountain massif; shares coordinates with the main high point.

wa_south_ridge.name: Andrews Creek Trailhead
wa_south_ridge.note: Shared Pasayten approach with Amphitheater Mountain massif.

wa_west_face.name: Andrews Creek Trailhead
wa_west_face.note: Shared Pasayten approach with Amphitheater Mountain massif.

wa_flight_of_the_falcon.name: Squire Creek Road Trailhead
wa_flight_of_the_falcon.note: Access from Darrington via Squire Creek Road to blocked parking; road washed out ~2 mi in.

wa_flight_of_the_falcon.name: Waterfall Basin (base of walls)
wa_flight_of_the_falcon.note: ~3-3.5 hr approach via old roadbed, streambed, and a Squire Creek ford into the basin below Three Fingers' east face.

wa_roan_wall_center_stage.name: Squire Creek Road Trailhead
wa_roan_wall_center_stage.note: Access from Darrington via Squire Creek Road to blocked parking; road washed out ~2 mi in.

wa_roan_wall_center_stage.name: Waterfall Basin (base of Roan Wall)
wa_roan_wall_center_stage.note: Two approach pitches (bolted 5.8 slab, then low-5th groove) lead to the base of the actual Roan Wall from this point.

wa_roan_wall_stage_right.name: Squire Creek Road Trailhead
wa_roan_wall_stage_right.note: Access from Darrington via Squire Creek Road to blocked parking; road washed out ~2 mi in.

wa_roan_wall_stage_right.name: Waterfall Basin (base of Roan Wall)
wa_roan_wall_stage_right.note: Two approach pitches (bolted 5.8 slab, then low-5th groove) lead to the base of the actual Roan Wall from this point.

wa_roofer_madness.name: Squire Creek Road Trailhead
wa_roofer_madness.note: Access from Darrington via Squire Creek Road to blocked parking; road washed out ~2 mi in.

wa_waterfall_buttress.name: Squire Creek Road Trailhead
wa_waterfall_buttress.note: Access from Darrington via Squire Creek Road to blocked parking; road washed out ~2 mi in.

wa_waterfall_buttress.name: Waterfall Basin (base of Waterfall Buttress)
wa_waterfall_buttress.note: Buttress sits left of the major slab waterfall; hike talus to the buttress toe. Base often buried under avalanche debris early season.

wa_unnamed_2.name: Washington Pass Hairpin Turn
wa_unnamed_2.note: Parking on the Hairpin Turn pullout on SR-20 below Washington Pass.

wa_unnamed_2.name: Spire Gully right - Alpenkuhl
wa_unnamed_2.note: ~7 minute walk up from the hairpin to this sunny, south-facing crag on Spire Gully's right side.

wa_unnamed_3.name: Washington Pass Hairpin Turn
wa_unnamed_3.note: Parking on the Hairpin Turn pullout on SR-20 below Washington Pass.

wa_unnamed_4.name: Washington Pass Hairpin Turn
wa_unnamed_4.note: Parking on the Hairpin Turn pullout on SR-20 below Washington Pass.

wa_unnamed_5.name: Washington Pass Hairpin Turn
wa_unnamed_5.note: Parking on the Hairpin Turn pullout on SR-20 below Washington Pass.

wa_unnamed_6.name: Washington Pass Hairpin Turn
wa_unnamed_6.note: Parking on the Hairpin Turn pullout on SR-20 below Washington Pass.

wa_beckey_davis.name: Snow Lakes Trailhead
wa_beckey_davis.note: Icicle Creek Rd approach into the Enchantments (Alpine Lakes Wilderness); overnight permit required.

wa_beckey_davis.name: Gnome Tarn
wa_beckey_davis.note: Landmark tarn on the boot path leading to the base of Prusik Peak's south face routes.

wa_beckey_davis.name: Prusik Peak
wa_beckey_davis.note: South Face route, 6-7 pitches, FA Beckey-Davis 1962.

wa_boving_christensen.name: Snow Lakes Trailhead
wa_boving_christensen.note: Icicle Creek Rd approach into the Enchantments (Alpine Lakes Wilderness); overnight permit required.

wa_energizer_bunny.name: Snow Lakes Trailhead
wa_energizer_bunny.note: Icicle Creek Rd approach into the Enchantments (Alpine Lakes Wilderness); overnight permit required.

wa_energizer_bunny.name: Prusik Peak
wa_energizer_bunny.note: South Face, left of Solid Gold, joins it near the top; 4-5 pitches.

wa_stanley_burgner.name: Snow Lakes Trailhead
wa_stanley_burgner.note: Icicle Creek Rd approach into the Enchantments (Alpine Lakes Wilderness); overnight permit required.

wa_blood_orgy.name: Hannegan Pass Trailhead
wa_blood_orgy.note: Start up Hannegan Pass Trail; after ~5 min leave it near a large white boulder and follow the climbers' path left into timber to Mamie Peak's crag.

wa_blood_orgy.name: Top of Mamie Peak wall
wa_blood_orgy.note: Blood Orgy is reached by rappel off Woodland Critter Christmas, or via a short 5.10 pitch starting up it; Mamie Peak's true summit (6,108') is easy scrambling above.

wa_ellation.name: Hannegan Pass Trailhead
wa_ellation.note: Start up Hannegan Pass Trail; after ~5 min leave it near a large white boulder and follow the climbers' path left into timber to the base of the wall.

wa_ellation.name: Top of Ellation
wa_ellation.note: 8-pitch, ~700' 5.11a (or 5.10 A0) up shallow grooves above the crag.

wa_hail_satan.name: Hannegan Pass Trailhead
wa_hail_satan.note: Start up Hannegan Pass Trail; after ~5 min leave it near a large white boulder and follow the climbers' path left into timber to the base of the wall.

wa_hail_satan.name: Top of Mamie Peak wall
wa_hail_satan.note: Multi-pitch line on the same crag as Ellation and Blood Orgy.

wa_woodland_critter_christmas.name: Hannegan Pass Trailhead
wa_woodland_critter_christmas.note: Start up Hannegan Pass Trail; after ~5 min leave it near a large white boulder and follow the climbers' path left into timber to the base of the wall.

wa_woodland_critter_christmas.name: Top of Mamie Peak wall
wa_woodland_critter_christmas.note: Access pitch that connects into Blood Orgy via rappel or continued 5.10 climbing.

wa_blood_sport.name: Alpental / Snow Lake Trailhead parking
wa_blood_sport.note: Park at the Alpental ski area lot; an unsigned climbers' path drops from near the Snow Lake TH into slide alder toward Guye Peak.

wa_blood_sport.name: Blood Sport crag
wa_blood_sport.note: Short (~50') isolated crag low on the mountain, just around the corner from the main West Face, near the base of South Rib.

wa_improbable_traverse.name: Alpental / Snow Lake Trailhead parking
wa_improbable_traverse.note: Park at the Alpental ski area lot; hike the talus cone beneath Guye's West Face to the start of roped climbing.

wa_improbable_traverse.name: Top of West Face traverse
wa_improbable_traverse.note: Caution: a Nov 2021 rockfall destroyed a ~30x40ft section of the traverse; it now involves unprotected 5.9-10- downclimbing.

wa_south_gully_south_spur.name: Alpental / Snow Lake Trailhead parking
wa_south_gully_south_spur.note: Park at the Alpental ski area lot; approach toward Guye Peak's south side.

wa_south_gully_south_spur.name: Guye Peak
wa_south_gully_south_spur.note: Ascend the obvious south-side gully past chockstones, moving onto the South Spur to the summit; climbable summer or winter.

wa_south_rib.name: Alpental / Snow Lake Trailhead parking
wa_south_rib.note: Park at the Alpental ski area lot; hike the Sahale ski track toward Guye Peak, then traverse boulder fields/slide alder to the cliff base.

wa_south_rib.name: Guye Peak
wa_south_rib.note: Low-5th-class rib climbing to the summit; a relatively easy technical way up Guye.

wa_concerto_in_c_for_drill_and_hammer.name: Squire Creek Trailhead (FS Rd 2040)
wa_concerto_in_c_for_drill_and_hammer.note: From Darrington, follow Squire Creek Rd past the Squire Creek Trail #654 sign; watch for two cut logs marking the boot-track that descends to ford the creek.

wa_concerto_in_c_for_drill_and_hammer.name: Grassy saddle (South Face routes)
wa_concerto_in_c_for_drill_and_hammer.note: ~3 hr approach through forest, a tributary crossing, and two gullies to the saddle below Squire Creek Wall's South Face; 14-pitch 5.8 friction slab leads to the summit crest above Buckeye Basin.

wa_doorway_flake.name: Squire Creek Trailhead (FS Rd 2040)
wa_doorway_flake.note: From Darrington, follow Squire Creek Rd past the Squire Creek Trail #654 sign; watch for two cut logs marking the boot-track that descends to ford the creek.

wa_doorway_flake.name: Grassy saddle (South Face routes)
wa_doorway_flake.note: Shares the same ~3 hr approach as the wall's other South Face routes.

wa_skeena26.name: Squire Creek Trailhead (FS Rd 2040)
wa_skeena26.note: From Darrington, follow Squire Creek Rd past the Squire Creek Trail #654 sign; watch for two cut logs marking the boot-track that descends to ford the creek.

wa_skeena26.name: Grassy saddle (South Face routes)
wa_skeena26.note: Brilliant white slab starting near the saddle, mostly 5.4-5.6 with a 5.9 opening pitch.

wa_western_dihedral.name: Squire Creek Trailhead (FS Rd 2040)
wa_western_dihedral.note: From Darrington, follow Squire Creek Rd past the Squire Creek Trail #654 sign; watch for two cut logs marking the boot-track that descends to ford the creek.

wa_western_dihedral.name: Grassy saddle (South Face routes)
wa_western_dihedral.note: Climbs the obvious reddish right-facing corner visible from the saddle; crux liebacking/stemming and a finger traverse on pitches 2-3.

wa_direct_finish.name: Hurricane Ridge Visitor Center
wa_direct_finish.note: Drive Obstruction Point Road ~2 mi from Hurricane Ridge to a pullout near Steeple Rock's basalt pillar.

wa_direct_finish.name: Steeple Rock
wa_direct_finish.note: Direct finish line to the summit block, typically combined with another route's lower pitches.

wa_east_ridge.name: Hurricane Ridge Visitor Center
wa_east_ridge.note: Drive Obstruction Point Road ~2 mi from Hurricane Ridge to a pullout near Steeple Rock.

wa_east_ridge.name: Steeple Rock
wa_east_ridge.note: 5.0 scramble along the S side of the ridge on the east end, up to the tree below the summit.

wa_north_ridge.name: Hurricane Ridge Visitor Center
wa_north_ridge.note: Drive Obstruction Point Road ~2 mi from Hurricane Ridge to a pullout near Steeple Rock.

wa_north_ridge.name: Steeple Rock
wa_north_ridge.note: Class 2-3 scramble up scree and heather on the west slope to the north ridgeline and summit.

wa_wings.name: Hurricane Ridge Visitor Center
wa_wings.note: Drive Obstruction Point Road ~2 mi from Hurricane Ridge to a pullout near Steeple Rock.

wa_wings.name: Steeple Rock
wa_wings.note: 5.8 up the northernmost crack on the W face: a left-facing corner to a belay atop a pillar, then a loose finish up a shallow chimney.

wa_ellen_pea.name: Highway 20 pullout near M&M Wall
wa_ellen_pea.note: Park roadside ~3 mi east of the Washington Pass hairpin; ascend talus directly uphill (north) toward the Supercave gully, ~1 hr to the lower ledge.

wa_ellen_pea.name: Upper ledge, M&M Wall
wa_ellen_pea.note: The 'Supercave' route: 8 pitches of 5.11c passing beneath the wall's huge signature cave; regarded as among the state's finest lines.

wa_the_exorcism_of_mark_hanna.name: Highway 20 pullout near M&M Wall
wa_the_exorcism_of_mark_hanna.note: Park roadside ~3 mi east of the Washington Pass hairpin; ascend talus directly uphill (north) toward the Supercave gully.

wa_the_exorcism_of_mark_hanna.name: Upper ledge, M&M Wall
wa_the_exorcism_of_mark_hanna.note: 5.11a trad line on the same south-facing wall.

wa_the_l_h_route_free_variation.name: Highway 20 pullout near M&M Wall
wa_the_l_h_route_free_variation.note: Park roadside ~3 mi east of the Washington Pass hairpin; ascend talus directly uphill (north) toward the Supercave gully.

wa_the_l_h_route_free_variation.name: Upper ledge, M&M Wall
wa_the_l_h_route_free_variation.note: 7-pitch 5.12 (7b+) free variation of the original L&H aid line.

wa_the_tiger.name: Highway 20 pullout near M&M Wall
wa_the_tiger.note: Park roadside ~3 mi east of the Washington Pass hairpin; ascend talus directly uphill (north) toward the Supercave gully, ~1 hr 10 min to the upper ledge.

wa_the_tiger.name: Upper ledge, M&M Wall
wa_the_tiger.note: 11-pitch 5.12 up the wall's south face, one of the longer lines on M&M Wall.

wa_brothers_traverse.name: Lena Lake Trailhead
wa_brothers_traverse.note: Follow Lower Lena Lake Trail #810 around the lake, then the Brothers Trail through the Valley of the Silent Men toward the South Couloir.

wa_brothers_traverse.name: The Brothers, South Peak (Mt. Edward)
wa_brothers_traverse.note: Traverse is usually done North Peak to South Peak: from the notch on South Brother's SE ridge, moderate snow/rock leads up a steep couloir to this summit.

wa_south_couloir.name: Lena Lake Trailhead
wa_south_couloir.note: Follow Lower Lena Lake Trail #810 around the lake, then the Brothers Trail through the Valley of the Silent Men; pick up a path along the west fork of Lena Creek to the South Couloir.

wa_south_couloir.name: The Brothers, South Peak (Mt. Edward)
wa_south_couloir.note: Ascend the South Couloir, exit west onto scree at ~5,000', traverse near cliffs, then pass the class-3 Hourglass to reach the summit; helmet/crampons/axe essential.

wa_north_face_var_right_directisimo.name: Blue Lake Trailhead
wa_north_face_var_right_directisimo.note: Signed trailhead on SR-20 ~1 mi west of Washington Pass; follow Blue Lake Trail then the climber's path up to the Concord-Lexington gully.

wa_north_face_var_right_directisimo.name: Concord Tower Summit
wa_north_face_var_right_directisimo.note: Shared summit for all Concord Tower routes; North Face follows the crack system directly up the face.

wa_south_face_3.name: Blue Lake Trailhead
wa_south_face_3.note: Signed trailhead on SR-20 ~1 mi west of Washington Pass; follow Blue Lake Trail then the climber's path up to the Concord-Lexington gully.

wa_south_face_3.name: Concord Tower Summit
wa_south_face_3.note: Shared summit for all Concord Tower routes.

wa_south_face_center.name: Blue Lake Trailhead
wa_south_face_center.note: Signed trailhead on SR-20 ~1 mi west of Washington Pass; follow Blue Lake Trail then the climber's path up to the Concord-Lexington gully.

wa_south_face_center.name: Concord Tower Summit
wa_south_face_center.note: Shared summit for all Concord Tower routes.

wa_the_cave_route.name: Blue Lake Trailhead
wa_the_cave_route.note: Signed trailhead on SR-20 ~1 mi west of Washington Pass; follow Blue Lake Trail then the climber's path up to the Concord-Lexington gully.

wa_the_cave_route.name: Concord Tower Summit
wa_the_cave_route.note: Shared summit; Cave Route is an alternative line used when Liberty Bell is crowded.

wa_classic_route_2.name: Snow Lake Trailhead
wa_classic_route_2.note: On Stevens Canyon Road ~1.5 mi east of Reflection Lakes, Mount Rainier NP; follow the Snow Lake trail then ascend the upper basin toward the saddle west of the peak.

wa_classic_route_2.name: Unicorn Peak Summit
wa_classic_route_2.note: Shared high point for all Unicorn Peak routes; easy 5th class/scramble finish.

wa_open_book_2.name: Snow Lake Trailhead
wa_open_book_2.note: On Stevens Canyon Road ~1.5 mi east of Reflection Lakes, Mount Rainier NP; follow the Snow Lake trail then ascend the upper basin toward the saddle west of the peak.

wa_open_book_2.name: Unicorn Peak Summit
wa_open_book_2.note: Shared high point for all Unicorn Peak routes.

wa_the_roof.name: Snow Lake Trailhead
wa_the_roof.note: On Stevens Canyon Road ~1.5 mi east of Reflection Lakes, Mount Rainier NP; follow the Snow Lake trail then ascend the upper basin toward the saddle west of the peak.

wa_the_roof.name: Unicorn Peak Summit
wa_the_roof.note: Shared high point for all Unicorn Peak routes.

wa_clast_from_the_past.name: Pinto Rock Pullout (North Side)
wa_clast_from_the_past.note: Short walk from the NF-77 pullout to the North Side wall, a lichen-streaked basalt band on Pinto Rock's north face; this route follows the band on the left.

wa_sidewinder_4.name: Pinto Rock Pullout (North Side)
wa_sidewinder_4.note: Short walk from the NF-77 pullout to the North Side wall; Sidewinder follows the lichen band to the right of Clast from the Past.

wa_top_gun.name: Pinto Rock Pullout (North Side)
wa_top_gun.note: Short walk from the NF-77 pullout to the North Side wall; Top Gun is toproped/lowered-into from the chains above Sidewinder's roof lip.

wa_diamond_in_the_rough.name: Bedal Creek/Sloan Creek Trailhead
wa_diamond_in_the_rough.note: End of FR-4096, ~3 mi off the Mountain Loop Highway south of Bedal Campground.

wa_diamond_in_the_rough.name: Sloan Peak Summit
wa_diamond_in_the_rough.note: Shared summit for Sloan Peak's technical routes.

wa_fire_on_the_mountain.name: Bedal Creek/Sloan Creek Trailhead
wa_fire_on_the_mountain.note: End of FR-4096, ~3 mi off the Mountain Loop Highway south of Bedal Campground.

wa_fire_on_the_mountain.name: Sloan Peak Summit
wa_fire_on_the_mountain.note: 7-pitch 5.10d route up the southwest face, topping out at the shared summit.

wa_northwest_buttress.name: Bedal Creek/Sloan Creek Trailhead
wa_northwest_buttress.note: End of FR-4096, ~3 mi off the Mountain Loop Highway south of Bedal Campground.

wa_northwest_buttress.name: Sloan Peak Summit
wa_northwest_buttress.note: Longest route on the peak; shared summit with good bivy spots along the buttress.

wa_easy_getaway.name: Cutthroat Creek/Lake Trailhead
wa_easy_getaway.note: On FR-400, 1 mi off SR-20 east of Washington Pass.

wa_easy_getaway.name: Cutthroat Wall Base
wa_easy_getaway.note: Base of the granite buttress via ~45-90 min of bushwhacking; giant step features mark the route starts.

wa_one_piece_at_a_time.name: Cutthroat Creek/Lake Trailhead
wa_one_piece_at_a_time.note: On FR-400, 1 mi off SR-20 east of Washington Pass.

wa_one_piece_at_a_time.name: Cutthroat Wall Base
wa_one_piece_at_a_time.note: Base of the granite buttress via ~45-90 min of bushwhacking.

wa_the_perfect_crime_with_variations.name: Cutthroat Creek/Lake Trailhead
wa_the_perfect_crime_with_variations.note: On FR-400, 1 mi off SR-20 east of Washington Pass.

wa_the_perfect_crime_with_variations.name: Cutthroat Wall Base
wa_the_perfect_crime_with_variations.note: Base of the granite buttress; descent/rappel gully navigation is the key crux of the day.

wa_first_amendment.name: Milepost 165 Pullout
wa_first_amendment.note: SR-20 turnout ~2 mi east of Washington Pass; climber's trail descends to cross Early Winters Creek before ascending to the formation.

wa_first_amendment.name: Le Petit Cheval Upper Mountain
wa_first_amendment.note: Shared upper section for Le Petit Cheval's multi-pitch routes.

wa_spontaneity_arete.name: Milepost 165 Pullout
wa_spontaneity_arete.note: SR-20 turnout ~2 mi east of Washington Pass; climber's trail descends to cross Early Winters Creek before ascending to the formation.

wa_spontaneity_arete.name: Le Petit Cheval Upper Mountain
wa_spontaneity_arete.note: 6-pitch 5.7 arete; shared topout with the other Le Petit Cheval routes.

wa_spontaneous_distraction.name: Milepost 165 Pullout
wa_spontaneous_distraction.note: SR-20 turnout ~2 mi east of Washington Pass; climber's trail descends to cross Early Winters Creek before ascending to the formation.

wa_spontaneous_distraction.name: Le Petit Cheval Upper Mountain
wa_spontaneous_distraction.note: Shared upper section for Le Petit Cheval's multi-pitch routes.

wa_iceman.name: Blue Lake Trailhead
wa_iceman.note: Same trailhead used for the Concord/Lexington cluster; from the climber's path, cut right into a dry creek bed and ascend fixed-line slabs to the Ice Box.

wa_iceman.name: Ice Box Left Side Base
wa_iceman.note: Base of the Ice Box wall, shared by Iceman, Route C, and Route D; approach slabs are smooth and slippery.

wa_route_c.name: Blue Lake Trailhead
wa_route_c.note: Same trailhead used for the Concord/Lexington cluster; from the climber's path, cut right into a dry creek bed and ascend fixed-line slabs to the Ice Box.

wa_route_c.name: Ice Box Left Side Base
wa_route_c.note: Base of the Ice Box wall, shared by Iceman, Route C, and Route D.

wa_route_d.name: Blue Lake Trailhead
wa_route_d.note: Same trailhead used for the Concord/Lexington cluster; from the climber's path, cut right into a dry creek bed and ascend fixed-line slabs to the Ice Box.

wa_route_d.name: Ice Box Left Side Base
wa_route_d.note: Base of the Ice Box wall, shared by Iceman, Route C, and Route D.

wa_koala_krack.name: Washington Pass Hairpin Turnout
wa_koala_krack.note: Park in the large turnout on the SR-20 hairpin switchback just east of Washington Pass; follow the boulder-field climber's trail south toward Kangaroo Pass.

wa_koala_krack.name: Kangaroo Temple
wa_koala_krack.note: Formation high point near the notch between Kangaroo Temple and Little Finger; Koala Krack is the easiest (5.4) line to the top.

wa_northwest_face_2.name: Washington Pass Hairpin Turnout
wa_northwest_face_2.note: Park in the large turnout on the SR-20 hairpin switchback just east of Washington Pass; follow the boulder-field climber's trail south toward Kangaroo Pass.

wa_northwest_face_2.name: Kangaroo Temple
wa_northwest_face_2.note: 5-pitch Northwest Face route tops out on the summit block.

wa_south_face_4.name: Washington Pass Hairpin Turnout
wa_south_face_4.note: Park in the large turnout on the SR-20 hairpin switchback just east of Washington Pass; follow the boulder-field climber's trail south toward Kangaroo Pass.

wa_south_face_4.name: Kangaroo Temple
wa_south_face_4.note: South Face (5.8 R) is runout in sections despite solid rock; tops out on the summit block.

wa_ne_couloir.name: Tiffany Springs Campground
wa_ne_couloir.note: Follow Trail #373 east past Tiffany Lake to Honeymoon Pass (~45 min), then traverse south to the base of the North Face.

wa_ne_couloir.name: Tiffany Mountain
wa_ne_couloir.note: NE Couloir is a moderate snow/scramble line (low 5th/AI1-2) up the north-facing gully to the summit slope.

wa_north_tower_ridge_route.name: Tiffany Springs Campground
wa_north_tower_ridge_route.note: Follow Trail #373 east past Tiffany Lake to Honeymoon Pass (~45 min), then traverse south to the base of the North Face.

wa_north_tower_ridge_route.name: Tiffany Mountain
wa_north_tower_ridge_route.note: 6-pitch 5.8 line up the tower's ridge crest; final pitches are 4th/3rd class walking to Tiffany's main summit slope.

wa_tower_arete.name: Tiffany Springs Campground
wa_tower_arete.note: Follow Trail #373 east past Tiffany Lake to Honeymoon Pass (~45 min), then traverse south to the base of the North Face.

wa_tower_arete.name: Tiffany Mountain
wa_tower_arete.note: Easier 4th-class arete alternative to the North Tower Ridge Route, joining the same upper ridge to the summit slope.

wa_prey.name: Osprey Wall Roadside Pullout
wa_prey.note: Small roadside pullout (2-3 cars); 5-min walk through woods to a boulder/scree field, then 15 min up a mossy ramp to the wall base. Prey (5.10+) climbs corners/overhangs/slabs to the wall's top.

wa_salish.name: Osprey Wall Roadside Pullout
wa_salish.note: Small roadside pullout (2-3 cars); 5-min walk through woods to a boulder/scree field, then 15 min up a mossy ramp to the wall base.

wa_traverse.name: Osprey Wall Roadside Pullout
wa_traverse.note: Small roadside pullout (2-3 cars); 5-min walk through woods to a boulder/scree field, then 15 min up a mossy ramp to the wall base.

wa_dirty_sanchez.name: Goose Egg Mountain East Cliffs
wa_dirty_sanchez.note: East of Rimrock Lake on the south side of Hwy 12; short direct approach from a highway pullout. Multi-pitch, loose in places, PG-runout — wear a helmet.

wa_ride_the_lightning_2.name: Goose Egg Mountain East Cliffs
wa_ride_the_lightning_2.note: East of Rimrock Lake on the south side of Hwy 12; short direct approach from a highway pullout, just right of Dirty Sanchez.

wa_east_face.name: Downey Creek Trailhead
wa_east_face.note: Remote 2-day approach: Downey/Bachelor Creek trail to Itswoot Ridge, then over the Dome-Chickamin col and across the Chickamin Glacier to the Gunsight Range.

wa_east_face.name: Middle Peak (Gunsight Range)
wa_east_face.note: East Face (5.10d) climbs 6-7 pitches of clean granite on Middle Gunsight; approach distance is approximate given the glaciated, multi-day approach.

wa_gunrunner.name: Downey Creek Trailhead
wa_gunrunner.note: Remote 2-day approach: Downey/Bachelor Creek trail to Itswoot Ridge, then over the Dome-Chickamin col and across the Chickamin Glacier to the Gunsight Range.

wa_gunrunner.name: Middle Peak (Gunsight Range)
wa_gunrunner.note: Gunrunner (IV 5.10-, 18 pitches) traverses all four Gunsight summits; Middle Peak is one of the four climbed en route. Distance is approximate given the remote, glaciated approach.

wa_east_face_variation.name: Alpental Parking Lot
wa_east_face_variation.note: Park at Alpental ski area (I-90 Exit 52); take the Source Lake trail, then head into the basin below The Tooth's east face.

wa_east_face_variation.name: The Tooth
wa_east_face_variation.note: Steeper winter variation on the east face (AI3-4/M4-5) reaching the same summit as the standard South Face route.

wa_northeast_slabs.name: Alpental Parking Lot
wa_northeast_slabs.note: Park at Alpental ski area (I-90 Exit 52); hike the main trail to Source Lake, then turn south up-valley (avoid summer talus due to avalanche hazard in winter conditions).

wa_northeast_slabs.name: The Tooth
wa_northeast_slabs.note: Moderate 5-pitch winter snow/ice line up the steep dihedral system to the North Ridge and summit.

wa_east_ridge_4.name: Goodell Creek Trailhead
wa_east_ridge_4.note: Approach via Goodell Creek Trail and climber's path into Terror Basin (~7.4 mi, +5800'), then cross the Terror Glacier to the base of Inspiration Peak's south face.

wa_east_ridge_4.name: Inspiration Peak
wa_east_ridge_4.note: Route starts at the base of the south face, gains a col, then follows the exposed East Ridge — including a classic 5.9 hand crack — to the summit.

wa_south_face_5.name: Goodell Creek Trailhead
wa_south_face_5.note: Approach via Goodell Creek Trail and climber's path into Terror Basin (~7.4 mi, +5800'), then cross the Terror Glacier to the base of Inspiration Peak's south face.

wa_south_face_5.name: Inspiration Peak
wa_south_face_5.note: Grade III 5.8 line following a ramp system, finishing through the 'Great Gash' near the top of the face.

wa_east_ridge_8.name: Reflection Lake Trailhead
wa_east_ridge_8.note: South side of Stevens Canyon Road; start of the Pinnacle Saddle Trail.

wa_east_ridge_8.name: Pinnacle Peak
wa_east_ridge_8.note: East Ridge climbs from the Castle-Pinnacle col up loose 3rd-4th class rock with short 5th-class steps to the summit.

wa_southwest_scramble.name: Reflection Lake Trailhead
wa_southwest_scramble.note: South side of Stevens Canyon Road; NPS-maintained Pinnacle Peak Trail begins here.

wa_southwest_scramble.name: Pinnacle Peak
wa_southwest_scramble.note: Trail gains the Pinnacle-Plummer saddle (~5,920 ft) then an unmaintained class 2-3 scramble climbs the southwest side to the summit.

wa_ephemeral.name: Ice Box Rim / Cougar Flats Overlook
wa_ephemeral.note: Reached via FSR 1204 to FSR 760; a rim-side trail or established rappel drops to the crag base.

wa_particulate_matter.name: Ice Box Rim / Cougar Flats Overlook
wa_particulate_matter.note: Reached via FSR 1204 to FSR 760; a rim-side trail or established rappel drops to the crag base.

wa_excavation_aka_the_crack.name: Shuksan Crag Pullout
wa_excavation_aka_the_crack.note: Roadside crag on the left side of unpaved Hannegan Pass Road, about 2 minutes' drive from Mt. Baker Highway (SR-542) near milepost 46.

wa_last_chance_for_gas_aka_tricky_start.name: Shuksan Crag Pullout
wa_last_chance_for_gas_aka_tricky_start.note: Roadside crag on the left side of unpaved Hannegan Pass Road, about 2 minutes' drive from Mt. Baker Highway (SR-542) near milepost 46. Bolts noted as old/rusty as of last report.

wa_fish_whistle.name: Sunrise Mine Trailhead
wa_fish_whistle.note: End of Forest Road 4065; follow Sunrise Mine Trail to Headlee Pass then around Sperry Peak to the Vesper/Sperry basin.

wa_fish_whistle.name: Vesper Peak north face
wa_fish_whistle.note: Fish & Whistle starts from a large tree ~50 ft left of True Grit and climbs 5 pitches (5.10-) up the north face to near the summit.

wa_true_grit_2.name: Sunrise Mine Trailhead
wa_true_grit_2.note: End of Forest Road 4065; follow Sunrise Mine Trail to Headlee Pass then around Sperry Peak to the Vesper/Sperry basin.

wa_true_grit_2.name: Vesper Peak north face
wa_true_grit_2.note: True Grit is a 5-pitch, 5.8 line up the north face, one of the original moderate routes on the wall.

wa_lane_peak_r1.name: Reflection Lake Pullout
wa_lane_peak_r1.note: Approach to the Lane Peak north face couloirs from Stevens Canyon Road.

wa_lane_peak_r1.name: Lane Peak - Zipper couloir
wa_lane_peak_r1.note: The Zipper is the left-leaning ~45-degree couloir at the center of the north face; ice axe/crampons required.

wa_lane_peak_r2.name: Reflection Lake Pullout
wa_lane_peak_r2.note: Approach to the Lane Peak north face couloirs from Stevens Canyon Road.

wa_lane_peak_r2.name: Lane Peak - The Fly couloir
wa_lane_peak_r2.note: The Fly is the broad, easiest couloir at the base of the face, trending right to a notch in the west ridge.

wa_narcos.name: Cutthroat Creek Trailhead
wa_narcos.note: Signed pullout ~4.5 mi east of Washington Pass on Hwy 20.

wa_narcos.name: Cutthroat Creek Wall / Little Liberty Bell
wa_narcos.note: No established trail; hike through woods then traverse rocky benches to the wall base, ~1-1.5 hrs. Narcos is a 5.9 trad, PG13 line.

wa_wright_pond.name: Cutthroat Creek Trailhead
wa_wright_pond.note: Signed pullout ~4.5 mi east of Washington Pass on Hwy 20.

wa_wright_pond.name: Cutthroat Creek Wall / Little Liberty Bell
wa_wright_pond.note: No established trail; hike through woods then traverse rocky benches to the wall base, ~1-1.5 hrs. Wright-Pond is a 5.11a/b trad, PG13 line.

wa_north_face_6.name: Lake Serene Trailhead
wa_north_face_6.note: Off US-2 near Index; approach via the Lake Serene trail then the rockslide on the lake's west side.

wa_north_face_6.name: Mount Index - North Peak
wa_north_face_6.note: Grade III 5.6-5.7 up loose rock/dirt via the NE spur and a right-curving gully. Summit coordinate approximate: North, Middle, and Main peaks sit within a tight ~0.5 mi summit cluster and could not be reliably distinguished by GPS.

wa_traverse_of_mount_index.name: Lake Serene Trailhead
wa_traverse_of_mount_index.note: Standard start point for all Mount Index climbing objectives.

wa_traverse_of_mount_index.name: Mount Index - North Peak
wa_traverse_of_mount_index.note: First summit, reached via the North Face route; traverse then continues south across Middle Peak (elevation/coordinates not reliably documented).

wa_traverse_of_mount_index.name: Mount Index - Main Peak
wa_traverse_of_mount_index.note: Final and highest summit of the classic 1950 Schoening-Beckey traverse; loose rock throughout and a serious multi-pitch descent. Peak coordinates are closely spaced and approximate.

wa_north_ridge_2.name: Hannegan Pass Trailhead
wa_north_ridge_2.note: End of Forest Road 32, off Mt. Baker Highway SR-542.

wa_north_ridge_2.name: Whatcom Pass
wa_north_ridge_2.note: Via the Chilliwack River Trail through Boundary, Copper Creek, US Cabin, and Graybeal camps.

wa_north_ridge_2.name: Whatcom Peak
wa_north_ridge_2.note: North Ridge climbed directly south from Whatcom Pass; generally the faster of the two standard routes.

wa_south_spur.name: Hannegan Pass Trailhead
wa_south_spur.note: End of Forest Road 32; alternate approach via the Chilliwack River Trail then the Easy Ridge climbers' path.

wa_south_spur.name: Whatcom Peak
wa_south_spur.note: Approach crosses Easy Ridge, Easy Peak, and the Imperfect Impasse to Perfect Pass (~6,300 ft), then snowfields and 3rd-class rock up the south face/spur.

wa_north_ridge_4.name: Thunder Creek Trailhead
wa_north_ridge_4.note: South end of Colonial Creek Campground on Hwy 20.

wa_north_ridge_4.name: Primus Peak
wa_north_ridge_4.note: Via McAllister Camp (~6 mi in), then the ridge below Tricouni Peak to the Borealis Glacier and Lucky Pass (7,200 ft) col, finishing up Primus's ridge.

wa_primus_peak_south_ridge.name: Thunder Creek Trailhead
wa_primus_peak_south_ridge.note: South end of Colonial Creek Campground on Hwy 20; an alternate southern approach via Eldorado Creek and the Eldorado ice cap is also used for this side.

wa_primus_peak_south_ridge.name: Primus Peak
wa_primus_peak_south_ridge.note: South side/McAllister Glacier aspect reached by continuing past Lucky Pass or via the Eldorado ice cap traverse; glacier travel gear required.

wa_north_ridge_7.name: Boston Basin Trailhead
wa_north_ridge_7.note: Small pullout on Cascade River Road opposite Johannesburg Mountain, ~22 mi from Marblemount.

wa_north_ridge_7.name: Aiguille de l'M
wa_north_ridge_7.note: From Boston Basin high camp, approach via the Forbidden Gully/Cat Scratch Gullies area then traverse to the notch right of the North Ridge (5.3-5.5, half-day from camp).

wa_south_ridge_6.name: Boston Basin Trailhead
wa_south_ridge_6.note: Small pullout on Cascade River Road opposite Johannesburg Mountain.

wa_south_ridge_6.name: Aiguille de l'M
wa_south_ridge_6.note: South Ridge (5.6) climbs the exposed skyline ridge visible from Boston Basin; classic half-day outing from high camp.

wa_northeast_face.name: Dingford Creek / Middle Fork Snoqualmie Trailhead
wa_northeast_face.note: Approach via Dutch Miller Gap Trail then cross-country toward Dutch Miller Gap and the peak.

wa_northeast_face.name: Little Big Chief - Summit
wa_northeast_face.note: NE Face is a 4th-class scramble with snow to the summit block.

wa_northwest_face_4.name: Dingford Creek / Middle Fork Snoqualmie Trailhead
wa_northwest_face_4.note: Approach via Dutch Miller Gap Trail then cross-country toward Dutch Miller Gap and the peak.

wa_northwest_face_4.name: Little Big Chief - Summit
wa_northwest_face_4.note: NW Face (Falcon Route), 5.9 trad line to the summit.

wa_playing_not_spraying.name: Cedar Creek Trailhead
wa_playing_not_spraying.note: Hike Cedar Creek Trail ~5mi, then a rough climbers path follows the creek draining the south side of Silver Horn.

wa_playing_not_spraying.name: The Silver Horn - Summit
wa_playing_not_spraying.note: South/SE face rock climb; rarely climbed sub-summit of Silver Star.

wa_the_chalice.name: Cedar Creek Trailhead
wa_the_chalice.note: Hike Cedar Creek Trail ~5mi, then a rough climbers path follows the creek draining the south side of Silver Horn.

wa_the_chalice.name: The Silver Horn - Summit
wa_the_chalice.note: The Chalice route on the SE face of Silver Horn.

wa_rampage.name: Bench Camp spring
wa_rampage.note: Fresh water at the Larch/Bench Camp below the Wine Spires cirque; reached via steep climbers trail from the SR-20 pullout near milepost 166.

wa_west_ridge_2.name: Bench Camp spring
wa_west_ridge_2.note: Fresh water at the Larch/Bench Camp below the Wine Spires cirque; reached via steep climbers trail from the SR-20 pullout near milepost 166.

wa_west_ridge_2.name: Paisano Pinnacle - Summit
wa_west_ridge_2.note: Classic 7-8 pitch West Ridge to the summit.

wa_se_ridge_aka_shield_wall.name: Mount Washington - Summit
wa_se_ridge_aka_shield_wall.note: SE Ridge / Shield Wall route, class 3+ scramble.

wa_washington_ellinor_traverse_ridge.name: Mount Washington - Summit
wa_washington_ellinor_traverse_ridge.note: Climb Washington first, then traverse the ridge SW toward Ellinor.

wa_washington_ellinor_traverse_ridge.name: Mount Ellinor Upper Trailhead
wa_washington_ellinor_traverse_ridge.note: Standard descent/exit point via Mount Ellinor's upper trailhead.

wa_south_twin_sister_north_ridge.name: South Twin Sister - Summit
wa_south_twin_sister_north_ridge.note: North Ridge route gains the ridge crest before a longer traverse to the summit.

wa_south_twin_sister_scramble.name: South Twin Sister - Summit
wa_south_twin_sister_scramble.note: Standard olivine scramble, more direct line than the ridge routes.

wa_southwest_ridge.name: Goodell Creek Trailhead
wa_southwest_ridge.note: Standard Southern Pickets approach via Goodell Creek and Terror Basin.

wa_southwest_ridge.name: West McMillan Spire - Summit
wa_southwest_ridge.note: Approximate summit position, anchored to the adjacent, well-documented (East) McMillan Spire summit in the same massif since no independently verified GPS fix for West McMillan Spire's summit was found.

wa_west_ridge_6.name: Goodell Creek Trailhead
wa_west_ridge_6.note: Standard Southern Pickets approach via Goodell Creek and Terror Basin.

wa_west_ridge_6.name: West McMillan Spire - Summit
wa_west_ridge_6.note: Approximate summit position, anchored to the adjacent, well-documented (East) McMillan Spire summit in the same massif since no independently verified GPS fix for West McMillan Spire's summit was found. West Ridge is the most popular Southern Picket route.

wa_apex_buttress.name: Thirtymile Trailhead
wa_apex_buttress.note: Via Chewuch Trail #510 then Tungsten Creek Trail #534.

wa_apex_buttress.name: Apex Mountain - Summit
wa_apex_buttress.note: Apex Buttress climbs the north wall above Tungsten Lake.

wa_big_snow_mountain_south_route.name: Snowflake Lake
wa_big_snow_mountain_south_route.note: Traverse around the lake before the final ascent.

wa_blue_s_buttress.name: Washington Pass Hairpin pullout (approx.)
wa_blue_s_buttress.note: Approximate — park at/near the Hairpin pullout on SR-20 just west of Washington Pass; exact pullout GPS not independently confirmed.

wa_blue_s_buttress.name: Poster Peak - Summit
wa_blue_s_buttress.note: Blue's Buttress, moderate 8-10 pitch route, short approach.

wa_clean_break.name: Silver Star Creek Sno-Park
wa_clean_break.note: Climbers trail up the east side of Silver Star Creek drainage, crossing at ~5000ft.

wa_clean_break.name: Juno Tower - Summit
wa_clean_break.note: Clean Break, 15-pitch route on the east walls above Silver Star Creek.

wa_e_se_face.name: Aasgard Pass
wa_e_se_face.note: Steep pass into the Enchantment basin.

wa_e_se_face.name: Witches Tower - Summit
wa_e_se_face.note: E/SE Face route; tower divides Snow Creek Glacier from the upper Enchantments.

wa_east_face_2.name: Silver Star Creek Sno-Park
wa_east_face_2.note: Approach via Silver Star Creek drainage or the Burgundy Col climbers trail.

wa_east_face_2.name: Chablis Spire - Summit
wa_east_face_2.note: East Face, easily attained summit; distinctive bunny-ears profile.

wa_east_face_3.name: Washington Pass parking (approx.)
wa_east_face_3.note: Park a few hundred yards east of Washington Pass near the small pond; exact pullout GPS not independently confirmed.

wa_east_face_3.name: Minuteman Spire - Summit
wa_east_face_3.note: East Face, 5-6 pitch route on the east side of the Liberty Bell group.

wa_east_ridge_2.name: Kindy Creek Trailhead
wa_east_ridge_2.note: Via Kindy Creek Road (FR-1570) off Cascade River Road.

wa_east_ridge_2.name: Cyclone Lake
wa_east_ridge_2.note: Common overnight bivy for the East Ridge route.
